text: Total daily caloric and protein intake over the long term are more important for exercise adaptations than nutrient timing.
Consuming protein pre- and/or post-workout induces a significant rise in muscle protein synthesis.
Peri-exercise protein intake, particularly post-training, can optimize physical performance and recovery for both resistance and endurance exercise once total daily intake is adequate.
The utility of post-workout protein feeding varies by training status, exercise duration, and number of training or competitive sessions per day.
Consuming nothing post-training offers no advantage over consuming protein and may represent a disadvantage for performance and recovery.
Athletes should consume protein post-training and post-competition as a prudent approach to support performance and recovery.
